ideas.title: Reuse current installer primitives
ideas.description: Keep `initFabric`, `installBootstrap`, `installMcpClients`, and `installHooks` as the execution layer and only add orchestration prompts above them.
ideas.rationale: This is the lowest-risk path and preserves test coverage.

ideas.title: Use prompt-driven CLI, not terminal app
ideas.description: If Fabric adds interactivity, prefer `inquirer` or `@clack/prompts` plus simple summary formatting.
ideas.rationale: Matches ccw's real implementation and avoids unnecessary architecture churn.

ideas.title: Separate CI-safe and TTY-safe paths
ideas.description: Wizard mode should be opt-in or TTY-only; non-TTY should stay deterministic and argument-driven.
ideas.rationale: Prevents install prompts from breaking automation.

key_findings: The current Fabric stack is intentionally lightweight and script-friendly.
The biggest risk is conflating onboarding UX with the automation-oriented init command.
challenged_assumptions: The current `interactive` summary in `fabric init` is not enough to qualify as a wizard.
open_questions: Would `@clack/prompts` offer a smaller surface than `inquirer` while still supporting the required flow?
If init is rewritten, should `--yes` become the new script-safe compatibility mode?
